text.title: django-imports Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for django-imports as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Direct cross-app model import: from apps.users.models import User.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: In projects with multiple apps each defining a User model, direct imports create silent name collisions. The alias convention makes the source app explicit at every usage and enables reliable tooling.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Wildcards make namespace scope opaque to tools (mypy, pylint, IDEs), prevent refactoring automation, and collapse the alias convention's disambiguation benefit.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Re-exports collapse the app namespace and recreate the original collision problem. Consumers can import from the wrong location silently. Keep __init__.py empty unless explicitly justified.

failure-mode.description: DRF serializer Meta.model gated under TYPE_CHECKING.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Serializer Meta.model is resolved at class creation time (import time). A TYPE_CHECKING-gated import makes the symbol undefined at runtime, causing an AttributeError on serializer load. Use apps.get_model() or a runtime import for Meta.model.

failure-mode.description: Agent rewrites import aliases but misses call sites: User.objects.get() not updated to user_models.User.objects.get().
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Partial rewrites leave dangling names that cause NameError at runtime. Alias fixes require AST-based rewrite of both the import and every usage, not just the import line.

failure-mode.description: Inconsistent alias names across files: user_models in one file, users_models in another.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Inconsistency defeats the readability benefit of the convention. Every new contributor must re-discover the alias for each app. Pin one suffix pattern (_models, _services, _constants) project-wide.
